title: 2025 Worldwide Box Office: Top-Grossing Films
text:
Based on Box Office Mojo data, here are the highest-grossing films globally in 2025:

Top 10 Films:

1. Ne Zha 2 - $2.27 billion (99% international revenue). This Chinese animated sequel became the year's highest-grossing film and made history as the first animated film to earn over $2 billion.

2. Zootopia 2 - $1.87 billion (77.1% international). Disney's animated sequel reunited detectives Nick and Judy for a new case. The film achieved the highest worldwide debut for an animated release and became the studio's top-performing animation title.

3. Avatar: Fire and Ash - $1.49 billion (72.8% international). James Cameron's sci-fi epic continued the Pandora saga with a reported budget exceeding $350 million, making it among the most expensive films ever produced.

4. Lilo & Stitch - $1.04 billion (59.2% international). Disney's live-action remake of the 2002 animated classic proved remake burnout concerns unfounded. The project prompted development of a sequel.

5. A Minecraft Movie - $960.4 million (55.8% international). Jack Black and Jason Momoa starred in this video game adaptation that became the second-highest-grossing video game film of all time, earning $424 million domestically.

6. Jurassic World Rebirth - $869.1 million (60.9% international). Scarlett Johansson led this franchise reboot, which abandoned previous continuity for a new storyline emphasizing survival horror over corporate spectacle.

7. Demon Slayer: Kimetsu No Yaiba Infinity Castle - $741.4 million (81.5% international). The anime film adaptation became the highest-grossing Japanese film in history, launching a theatrical trilogy to conclude the series.

8. How to Train Your Dragon - $636.8 million (58.7% international). DreamWorks' live-action remake featured practical puppetry combined with detailed CGI.

9. F1: The Movie - $634.1 million (70.1% international). Brad Pitt and Damson Idris starred in this original sports drama—notably the only non-franchise film among the top ten.

10. Superman - $618.7 million (42.7% international). James Gunn's DC reboot, starring David Corenswet, earned $354 million domestically. The film's success prompted Warner Bros. to greenlight a sequel for 2027.

Key Insights: The data reveals that international markets dominate 2025 box office returns, with most top films generating 50-99% of revenue from overseas audiences. Animated films and major franchise sequels lead the year's earnings.
